ab: wa minokay to ko safa to fa’inayan anini to kakeremam.
ch: 今天傍晚，弟弟就要回家了。
en: My younger brother is going home this evening.

ab: tayra kako aci mamo i cifa a mi’aca to koko’, misaday to ’aredetay a sakalafi to hano safa.
ch: 我和奶奶去市場買雞，準備煮好吃的晚餐給弟弟。
en: My grandma and I went to the market to buy chickens and prepared a delicious dinner for my younger brother.

ab: mata’elif niyam ko paliwalay to dateng, adihay ko ko^ngelay a dateng to papaliwalen no paliwalay.
ch: 我們經過了菜攤，菜攤有好多新鮮的青菜。
en: We passed the vegetable stand, which had a lot of fresh green vegetables.

ab: o incay, korto ato ningcing ko mi’acaan niyam.
ch: 我們買了空心菜、山藥和胡蘿蔔。
en: We bought water spinach, yams and carrots.

ab: sowal sa koya tatihiay a tawki toya paliwalay to losay, “ko^sanay ko talacay ato lopas no mako, ’acaen ko pinapina makena!”
ch: 旁邊水果攤的老闆說:「我的鳳梨和桃子很甜。買一些吧!」
en: The owner of the nearby fruit stand said: “My pineapples and peaches are very sweet. Buy some!”

ab: ’acaan to niyam ko talacay ato lopas.
mata’elif niyam ko paliwalay to titi, sowal sa koya tawki, “o manan ko ’a’acaen no miso? ta’angayay maso^so aca koni a koko’ anini, tolo a so’ot ko ’aca no cecay.”
ch: 我們經過了肉攤，老闆說:「你要買什麼?今天的雞又大又肥，一隻300元。」
en: We passed the meat stall, and the boss said, “What do you want to buy? Today’s chickens are big and fat, and one is 300 NTD.”

ab: ’atekakan nanay ci mamo, manga’ay a saka’emeden to mamang han nira koya tawki a milicay.
ch: 奶奶覺得太貴了，問老闆可不可以便宜一點。
en: Grandma thought it was too expensive and asked the boss if it could be cheaper.

ab: nikawrira, ka’emeday to, caay to kanga’ay a lowanen sa ko sowal noya tawki.
ch: 但老闆說已經很便宜，不能再減了。
en: But the boss said that it is already very cheap and cannot be reduced any further.

ab: pasowal sa koya tatihiay a tawki, “katayni a mi’aca to foting ako, ta’angayay ka’emeday aca ko foting ako.”
ch: 隔壁的老闆說，來買我的魚，我的魚又大又便宜。
en: The boss next door said, come and buy my fish, my fish is big and cheap.

ab: ikor to i, ma’aca to niyam ko koko’ ato foting, minokay a tahaloma’ i, tangsol sa a mitangtang to cecay a ’eto a kakaenen.
ch: 最後我們買了雞也買了魚，回家煮了一桌子的菜。
en: In the end we bought chicken and fish, and went home to cook a table of food.

ab: halafinay to ko pitatala, to’eman sa to ko romi’ad.
en: After waiting for a long time, it was already dark.

ab: sa to’eman sa to ko romi’ad to pitatalaan.
en: After waiting for a long time, it was already dark.

ab: patingwa ko safa a pasowal, safana’sa a awa ko ’aliwac no sofitay sa, roma sa to a lipay to pa’aliwacan nga’ manga’ay a minokay saan.
ch: 弟弟打電話回來說，軍隊突然不能放假了，要下一個禮拜日才回來。
en: The younger brother called back and said that the army suddenly couldn’t take a vacation and would not be back until the next Sunday.

ab: pasowal sa ci mamo, “adihayay to ko mitangtangan, tahidangen to ko ’alomanay a mitahka.”
ch: 奶奶說已經煮了這麼多，叫大家一起來吃吧!
en: Grandma said that she has cooked so much, so let’s eat together!

ab: matahidang to niyam ko salawinawina ato widawidang a malacecay a mitahka tona kadofahay a sakalafi.
ch: 我們邀請了親朋好友，一起來享用豐盛的晚餐。
en: We invited friends and family to have a great dinner together.
